q: The burning wood in the air releases ___ and light energies.
The fireworks display illustrates a spectacular release of energy ($light$ and $heat$) in a ___ reaction.
___ is the chemical reaction that occurs when plants use energy from the sun, carbon dioxide, and water to synthesise the food that the plant needs to survive.
When we burn ___ for energy, chemical changes take place during the reaction and release huge amounts of energy.
Except for the energy produced from nuclear reactors, all of man's energy resources are chemicals or involve the ___ of energy through chemical reactions.
Energy changes are central to chemical reactions and understanding the role of energy in ___ provides for our daily life.
Whenever a chemical reaction (chemical change) occurs, ___ is usually transferred to or from the surroundings.
The energy change (gain or loss) during the reaction can be ___.
Changing ___ of matter (physical change) also involve energy changes.
___ (e.g., boiling water), melting (e.g., melting ice), condensation... are some examples of changing states around us in everyday life.
Mothball and dry ice ___ without melting.
There are various kinds of energy such as ___ energy (due to the movement).
___ energy is due to the position.
Energy can neither be created nor destroyed as stated in the ___.
Energy can change from one form to another without a net loss or gain of energy and it is called ___.
Electric motor converts electrical energy into ___ energy.
Hair dryer converts electrical energy into ___ energy.
___ converts chemical energy into electrical energy.
Automobile engine converts chemical energy into ___ energy.
Solar cell converts light energy into ___ energy.
All chemical reactions involve ___ changes.
___ is the energy that is stored in the bonds of chemical compounds such as atoms and molecules.
Humans release chemical energy during ___ and use it to power systems inside the body.
Plants store chemical energy in ___ formed during photosynthesis.
Whenever a chemical reaction takes place between reactants to form products, there is always an energy change usually in the form of ___.
Chemical reactions often involve changes in energy due to the ___ and formation of bonds.
The energy change is due to the difference in the amounts of ___ between the reactant and the product particles in a chemical reaction.
The interactions between particles can produce ___ or heating effects that are used in everyday applications.
A chemical reaction involves the breaking of bonds in the ___ and the forming of bonds in the products.
Energy is absorbed to break bonds, and energy is ___ when bonds are formed during the process of dissolving.
Chemical reactions provide ___ which is the result of interactions between ions in an electrolyte.

Some reactions take place on their own under a given set of conditions, and some are needed to provide energy for the reaction to proceed. A process that can take place on its own accord is called ___.
___ processes need the energy to take place.
Adding a solution of dilute sulphuric acid to a solution of ___ is an example of a spontaneous reaction.
The reaction between magnesium and oxygen, and the reaction between copper and oxygen are both ___ reactions.
___ is exothermic and spontaneous.
When the ice melts at room temperature, that is a spontaneous and ___ process.
When sulphuric acid is slowly poured into water, the flask or beaker becomes hot. This is heat being released to the ___ by the reacting system.
Heat being released to the surroundings by the reacting system is known as an ___ reaction.
Examples of exothermic reactions are burning candle, the combustion of a fuel, the neutralisation reaction, respiration, and ___.
Exothermic reactions give out energy. So, there is a ___ rise.
In exothermic reactions, the energy of the product is ___ than the energy of the reactants because energy has been released during the reaction.
When ammonium chloride is dissolved in water, the flask becomes quite ___ to the touch.
Heat being absorbed from the surroundings by the reacting system is known as an ___ reaction.
Examples of endothermic reactions are melting of ice, cooking an egg, evaporating liquid water, and ___ of limestone.
Endothermic reactions take in energy from the surroundings. So, there is a ___ fall.
In endothermic reactions, the energy of the products is ___ than the energy of the reactants because energy has been absorbed during the reaction.
Energy changes in chemical reactions are caused by the ___ and breaking of chemical bonds.
Bonds in the reactants must be ___ first.
Bond breaking is an ___ process.
Energy is ___ when new bonds form.
Bond making is an ___ process.
In an exothermic reaction: total energy absorbed for bond breaking $<$ ___.
c: total energy released for bond making

q: In an endothermic reaction: total energy absorbed for bond breaking $>$ ___.
c: total energy released for bond making

q: ___ is the specific part of the universe that contains the reaction or process which is studied.
___ are everything in the universe other than the system.
